profile.name: Lin
profile.concern: Should I push for graduate school or enter industry directly?
profile.objective: Trade off optionality, income ramp, and personal sustainability.

graph_nodes.label: Current Self
graph_nodes.description: Decision owner balancing research upside, employability, and sustainability.
graph_nodes.name: Lin
graph_nodes.summary: The main actor keeps both graduate-school and industry branches alive.

graph_nodes.label: Mentor Agent
graph_nodes.description: Mentor support is high but conditional on visible output quality.
graph_nodes.name: Lab Mentor
graph_nodes.summary: Recommendation quality is the main unlock for the research-first branch.

graph_nodes.label: Family Constraint
graph_nodes.description: Family can support ambition, but rising uncertainty increases pressure.
graph_nodes.name: Family System
graph_nodes.summary: Cash-flow visibility strongly affects branch stability.

graph_nodes.label: Employer Market
graph_nodes.description: Dense job market with strong learning upside and fast competition cycles.
graph_nodes.name: AI Product Market
graph_nodes.summary: High-city jobs reward shipping speed more than credential depth.

graph_nodes.label: City Gradient
graph_nodes.description: The city amplifies opportunity density and cost pressure at the same time.
graph_nodes.name: Shanghai Opportunity Field
graph_nodes.summary: Opportunity and burn rate move together in the chosen geography.

graph_nodes.label: Education State
graph_nodes.description: Tracks research preparation, application readiness, and signal strength.
graph_nodes.name: Education Axis
graph_nodes.summary: The education axis is strong enough to justify keeping research alive.

graph_nodes.label: Finance State
graph_nodes.description: Measures runway, cost pressure, and the urgency of short-term income.
graph_nodes.name: Cash Runway
graph_nodes.summary: Finance is the primary drag against the graduate-school branch.

graph_nodes.label: Mental State
graph_nodes.description: Tracks stress accumulation, recovery ability, and sustainability risk.
graph_nodes.name: Mental Load
graph_nodes.summary: Optionality is preserved, but attention now has a real carrying cost.

graph_nodes.label: Primary Fork
graph_nodes.description: The director keeps a three-branch frontier instead of collapsing to one answer.
graph_nodes.name: Graduate vs Industry Fork
graph_nodes.summary: This node anchors branching, counterfactual edits, and future cycle expansion.

graph_nodes.label: Future Self
graph_nodes.description: Represents a future self that values compounding growth with manageable regret.
graph_nodes.name: 3-Year Self
graph_nodes.summary: The graph ties each branch back to long-horizon meaning, not only short-term utility.

agents.name: Self Agent
agents.stance: Bias toward optionality with controlled downside.

agents.name: Relationship Agent
agents.stance: Supports research growth when output quality remains visible.

agents.name: Family Agent
agents.stance: Accepts ambition, but resists long uncertainty arcs.

agents.name: Institution Agent
agents.stance: Rewards fast entry and practical shipping velocity.

agents.name: Director Agent
agents.stance: Keeps only informative branches and writes each cycle back into memory.

paths.title: Research First
paths.thesis: Delay earnings ramp to maximize long-term option value and academic leverage.
paths.summary: Best when mentor signal remains explicit, memory writeback keeps stress visible, and the branch does not hide finance pressure. Cycle 2 keeps Research First in front because optionality remains the cleanest compounding axis while the engine watches mentor, education-state, decision-core. nodes.title: Mentor commits to recommendation
nodes.summary: A concrete lab signal makes graduate applications credible.

editable_fields.label: Mentor Signal
editable_fields.rationale: Recommendation quality is the main branch unlock.

nodes.title: Application cycle compresses time
nodes.summary: Sleep and side projects narrow as application load rises.

nodes.title: Graduate admit unlocks specialized path
nodes.summary: Short-term uncertainty converts into deeper research positioning.

immediate: Lock one publishable project with a visible milestone inside six weeks.
Turn mentor support into a dated recommendation commitment.
next_quarter: Run an internship hedge in parallel with applications.
Track stamina weekly; this branch fails when health debt becomes invisible.
risk_controls: Set a pivot deadline if recommendation quality slips.
Keep a deployable portfolio package ready for direct applications.
questions_to_track: Is mentor support still specific or becoming rhetorical?
Has finance pressure crossed the branch's tolerance line?

detail_fields.last_director_note: Cycle 6 keeps Research First in front because optionality remains the cleanest compounding axis while the engine watches mentor, education-state, decision-core.
detail_fields.last_counterfactual_note: The edited branch Research First shifts around mentor; the engine treats mentor_signal, strong, rf-1 as the main reason to rebalance confidence and risk.

paths.title: Pragmatic Employment
paths.thesis: Choose a stable early role, reduce uncertainty, and optimize for execution quality from a calmer base.
paths.summary: Safest when family stability and short-term runway need clean answers, but it must guard against comfort turning into low-growth drift.

nodes.title: Local stable offer accepted
nodes.summary: A lower-volatility role immediately improves certainty and family alignment.

nodes.title: Learning slope needs intentional design
nodes.summary: The branch drifts if you trade all ambition for comfort.

nodes.title: Calm base enables focused upgrade
nodes.summary: Stable execution creates room for a more deliberate next pivot.

immediate: Prioritize manager quality and runway over prestige labels.
Negotiate for ownership so the branch still compounds skill.
next_quarter: Use lower volatility to build one durable differentiator after work.
Review whether comfort is becoming stagnation at the six-month mark.
risk_controls: Do not let short-term relief erase long-term compounding.
Keep one structured reskilling lane active.
questions_to_track: Is the stable branch preserving enough optionality?
Has certainty started to suppress ambition instead of supporting it?

paths.title: Hybrid City Leap
paths.thesis: Use a high-density city role to compound market knowledge while preserving study optionality.
paths.summary: Highest upside when city burn stays under control and shipped work compounds into future optionality instead of pure exhaustion.

nodes.title: Shanghai role offer lands
nodes.summary: A strong platform team offers immediate market immersion.

nodes.title: City cost pressure rises
nodes.summary: High burn challenges savings and energy unless routines stabilize fast.

nodes.title: Hybrid credibility compounds
nodes.summary: This branch creates both shipped work and a believable pivot story.

immediate: Target roles with mentorship and real shipping slope.
Map city burn rate before optimizing headline compensation.
next_quarter: Reserve weekly study time so the graduate hedge stays real.
Use shipped work as both promotion proof and future application evidence.
risk_controls: Avoid roles that consume all slack in the first 90 days.
Keep housing and commute below the stress threshold.
questions_to_track: Is city density improving social capital faster than it drains energy?
Is runway stable enough to keep optionality honest?
